sections.header: Verses 1–3 — “Save Me, O God, by Your Name”

heb.paragraph: "Save me, O God, by your name (shēm); vindicate me by your might" (v.1). The Ziphites betrayed David’s hiding place to Saul (1 Sam 23:19). David appeals to God’s name — his character, reputation, and power — as the instrument of salvation. Strangers attack; ruthless people seek his life (v.3).

notes.note: "Save me by your name." The name IS the power. David does not ask God to USE power; he asks God’s name to BE the salvation.

heb.paragraph: "Surely God is my help; the Lord is the one who sustains (sōmēk̅) me" (v.4). The pivot: from danger to confidence. God will repay the enemies for their treachery (v.5). David will sacrifice willingly and praise God’s name (v.6). "He has delivered me from all my troubles" (v.7) — past tense, spoken in faith before the rescue is complete.

notes.note: "Surely God is my help." The confidence word "surely" (ḥinnēh) marks the lament’s turn.
"I will sacrifice a freewill offering to you. He has delivered me." The vow of thanksgiving: when God rescues, David will worship.

ppl.name: David
ppl.text: " The vow of thanksgiving: when God rescues, David will worship…

ppl.name: God
ppl.text: he asks God’s name to BE the salvation…

chapter_panels.trans: VersionTranslationNIVI will sacrifice a freewill offering to you; I will praise your name, Lord, for it is good.ESVWith a freewill offering I will sacrifice to you; I will give thanks to your name, O LORD, for it is good.NIVArrogant foes are attacking me; ruthless people are trying to kill me— people without regard for God.ESVFor strangers have risen against me; ruthless men seek my life; they do not set God before themselves. Selah

rec.text: ""Save me by your name." The name IS the power. David does not ask God to USE power; he asks God’s name to BE the salvation."

rows.label: The Betrayal — 54:1–3
rows.text: "Save me by your name." The Ziphites betray David. Strangers attack.

hebtext.note: "Save me, O God, by your name (shēm); vindicate me by your might" (v.1). The Ziphites betrayed David’s hiding place to Saul (1 Sam 23:19). David appeals to God’s name — his character, reputation, and power — as the instrument of salvation. Strangers attack; ruthless people seek his life (v.3).

"Surely God is my help; the Lord is the one who sustains (sōmēk̅) me" (v.4). The pivot: from danger to confidence. God will repay the enemies for their treachery (v.5). David will sacrifice willingly and praise God’s name (v.6). "He has delivered me from all my troubles" (v.7) — past tense, spoken in faith before the rescue is complete.

themes.note: Faith: confidence in God’s name as instrument of rescue. Worship: the vow of freewill offering.
